content:
# Top 5 posts from r/MovieSuggestions day

## 1. Primer (2004) is a genius, low-budget sci-fi that demands your full attention.

**Score:** 120
**Body:** This is one of the most meticulously crafted time-travel movies ever made. It was shot on a tiny budget but feels incredibly smart and complex.  
You'll need to watch it more than once to piece everything together, and that's the best part of the experience.

### Top 5 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. I watched this once, thought I was following it, then in a moment had no idea what was happening
  - Being an edgy teen i was like “i fully understand this movie”. Then i opened the graphic someone made of all the timelines and i was oblivious to 95% of them lol

One of my first big humblings in life 
  - I've seen it more than once, but same thing
    - I've seen it like, 6 times, watched youtube videos explaining it, studied infographics. . . and yeah, same thing
      - My favorite review of this film said "I don't want to be spoon-fed, but at least unlock the kitchen door for me!"
  - it's pretty much impossible to follow. i will preface by saying i think primer is the best time travel movie ever made, but it's biggest strength is also it's biggest flaw; it's just too complex. as it should be, multiverse time travel theory is crazy. it also means the movie has great rewatch value, but you pretty much need to either watch an explanation after or take notes while watching and really scrutinize every word. the script is very tight and fast so every line of dialogue has purpose, and they don't ever give you a moment to breathe and digest, which just makes it even harder to follow. when you do get it though, it is so rewarding.
2. The dvd with the director commentary is key to understanding that movie. I love it 
3. One of the greatest science fiction kicked ever made. Doubly impressive that is was made for only $7000.

Primer was the only movie which I've ever rewatched immediately after watching it for the first time. Then I watched it three more times. After all that I think I understand about 80% of it.
4. The director was on his way to being a new Kubrick IMHO, but ended up stalking his girlfriend/starlet and has since disappeared. If I'm wrong does anyone have updates on what he's involved in?
  - He did Upstream Color in 2013. I haven't seen it but I know about it

## 2. Need movie recs that actually stick with you

**Score:** 32
**Body:** Alright, I’m in the mood for something that hits, not just entertaining, but the kind of movie that lingers in your head after the credits roll. Doesn’t matter if it’s drama, sci-fi, romance, or even horror, as long as it’s got depth, great writing, or strong characters.



Something that makes you go, “damn, that was good.”



What’s a movie like that for you?

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. A Place Beyond the Pines
2. Children if Men has stuck with me for so long.
3. The Usual Suspects 

Se7en

Come And See

Blade Runner

Heat

Stand By Me

The Legend Of 1900

Her

Goonies

Ex Machina

Old Boy

Drive

Inglorious Basterds
4. Amelie *(2001)*

Cabaret *(1972)*

Carrie *(1976)*

Come Back to the 5 and Dime, Jimmy Dean *(1982)*

Heavenly Creatures *(1994)*

Invasion of the Body Snatchers *(1978)*

To Die For *(1995)*
5. Gran Torino
6. Barbie

The Substance

Blue Valentine

The Purple Rose of Cairo

The Descendants 

Captain Fantastic

La La Land

If Only / Sana Dati

Miracle / Himala

Gone Girl

Moonlight

Sinners

Weapons
7. Midsommar 
8. Magnolia
9. LA Confidential

Almost Famous

The Secret Life of Walter Mitty

Dave
10. “Dr. Strangelove or How I Learned To Stop Worrying And Love The Bomb”

## 3. Looking for movies where the HERO and the VILLAIN team up to take on an even BIGGER threat — any recs?

**Score:** 32
**Body:** Hey everyone — I’m after movies where the protagonist and their usual antagonist/arch-villain end up joining forces (even if it’s an uneasy/temporary alliance) to fight a greater enemy or threat.  
A few rules for replies, please:

* The hero and villain must actually *team up* (not just share a common goal from afar).
* Prefer feature films (not TV episodes), but TV suggestions are fine if they’re iconic.
* If you can, include the **title**, **year**, and a one-line explanation of how they team up.

Examples welcome but please **no spoilers** beyond the teaming-up premise. Thanks — excited to see what you all come up with!

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Enemy Mine (1985)

Human and alien fighter pilots at war both crashland on the same planet and have to survive together.
2. I love the gangsters at the end of the Rocketeer. They’re criminals, but PATRIOTIC criminals!
  - Similar scene in Eraser, when the “union boys” come up against the international terrorists:

Tony Two Toes: There they are. Commie bastards!
Mikey: They're not communists any more, Tony. They're a federation of independent liberated states.
Tony Two Toes: Don't make me hurt you, Mikey.
3. X2: X-Men United (2003)

>!Magnrto helps the X-Men deal with Stryker, the guy who made Wolverine what he is, who is using his own mutant son to target other mutants.!<
4. Blade 2
5. Dusk Till Dawn.  Kidnappers and kidnapped join forces against..
6. Assault on Precinct 13 (1976 and 2005). People trapped inside of a police station team up to fight a criminal gang besieging said police station.
7. Predators (2010)

Royce and Predator team up. Near the end of the movie, since you don't want spoilers ill leave it at that.

Alien vs Predator (2004)

Lex teams up with Predator.
8. Godzilla vs kong... Came to mind at first.

Everything else i try to think i only come up with anime 😂

Maybe the new predator badlands movie could fit to this gategory also?
9. Fast & Furious franchise.
Basically the sequel would always have the previous villain return and help Toretto and the crew fight a badder villain.

## 4. Serial killer

**Score:** 28
**Body:** Looking for non-true crime related serial killer movies. I feel as though that’s all that gets recommended anymore after the Monster Netflix series came out. Any recommendations?

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Natural Born Killers. Buckle your seatbelt and enjoy the free fall
2. The House That Jack Built! It's a crazy movie! Kalifonia is good as well!
3. Seven ,Silence of the lambs,The girl with the dragon tattoo
4. Copycat,  of course there's Silence of the Lambs, if you want to go outside the country, I Saw the Devil is really good.
5. [Maniac](https://www.rottentomatoes.com/m/maniac_1980) (1981)

[Maniac](https://www.rottentomatoes.com/m/maniac_2013) (2013)

The original, and its remake, are both awesome.
6. YOU season 1 and 2 is good. I became a fan so I watched it up to the last season. but the 1st 2 seasons are the best
7. Silence of the Lambs
8. Kalifornia,Red Dragon, Mr Brooks,
9. M (1931) Directed by Fritz Lang and starring Peter Lorre.  It is a classic movie worth watching.
10. Not a movie but the Dexter series

## 5. Please, please, a movie that gives me hopes

**Score:** 24
**Body:** Please, people. I would really appreciate suggestions for a movie that gives me hopes back. My daughter keeps coming back with her possessive and controlling boyfriend. Need something to try and keep my mind away for a bit.

Thanks in advance.

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. The secret life of Walter Mitty
2. The Shawshank Redemption (1994)
3. The Peanut Butter Falcon 

The Sound of Music 

Chitty Chitty Bang Bang

Ernest Goes to Camp
4. Marcel the shell with shoes on or my life as a courgette
5. School of Rock
6. Tender Mercies
7. Moonstruck.
8. Life Of Chuck
9. Paddinton 2
10. My Neighbor Totoro

# Top 5 posts from r/moviecritic day

## 1. What is your favorite scene where the main villain becomes a secondary villain?

**Score:** 1124
**Body:** Scene in Deadpool & Wolverine. You think Mr. Paradox is the main antagonist? Cassandra Nova makes it clear that you're wrong when she grabs Paradox by the head and drags him down the stairs.

### Top 8 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Batman Begins. The R’as al Ghul reveal is great.
  - They did the same move in the third Nolen Batman movie.
2. When Darth Vader kneels before Palpatine in Return of the Jedi. 

If Darth Vader kneels to someone and calls them "my master", you should be scared of them.
3. “The Emperor is not as forgiving as I am.”
4. When Calvin Candy gets it and his most tenured slave becomes the new big bad
5. I don't know that this counts, but Charles Dance in the Last Action hero sort of promotes himself

[https://youtu.be/g751-jjKXC0?si=-OTc\_HD6joUUbhwS](https://youtu.be/g751-jjKXC0?si=-OTc_HD6joUUbhwS)
  - That movie doesn’t get the mass love it so desperately deserves!  This and True Lies were absolute peak Schwarzenegger films!
6. The Lost Boys when Edward Hermann is revealed to be the Lead Vampire. 

Kiefer Sutherland’s character just died, but Jason Patric was still “turned”, you’re watching like “wha thuh fuh?” And then the mom comes home from her date and yep, the Frog Brothers were right all along.
7. When Killmonger shoots Klaw dead to take his body to offer the tribe and get into Wakanda in Black Panther 1
  - The villain arcs in that movie are all pretty great.  Klaw probably the only through and through bad guy in the movie.  At that point he’s essentially the third villain behind  Umbaku (who becomes a good guy) and Klaw.

## 2. Which scene put an actress on the map?

**Score:** 764
**Body:** This one when I first saw Transformers. 

### Top 9 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Cameron Diaz, The Mask
  - Yep, her very first scene was star making.
2. Margot Robbie in Wolf of Wallstreet
3. Catherine Zeta-Jones arrives in California - The Mask of Zorro
4. Kathy bates-misery
5. Phoebe Cates in Fast Times at Ridgemont High
6. Alexandra Daddario in True Detective
  - > I didn’t expect to have what I had happen happen, which was - it’s a long time ago now - getting naked on a show where I didn’t have a huge role, I didn’t know what to expect. And then of course that episode aired and my manager called, and I’ll never forget it, I was sitting on my boyfriend’s couch and she calls me in the morning after the episode aired and she was like, ‘The phone’s been ringing off the hook all morning. The phone won’t stop ringing!’ And all of a sudden, everyone in town wanted to meet with me and then I booked San Andreas.

https://collider.com/true-detective-alexandra-daddario-interview/
7. Elisha Cuthbert when she said "I'm all wet. Can I come in?" in Girl Next Door
8. Tim Curry in RHPS

## 3. This scene in "Die Hard with a Vengeance" where Zeus (Samual L. Jackson) solves that riddle while John McClane (Bruce Willis) stands there completely useless bc he is so hungover is absolutely hilarious.

**Score:** 696
**Body:** I absolutely love the 3rd "Die Hard" movie. It really is a classic, and is filled with so many good scenes. Zeus saving John McClane's ass by solving that riddle bc McClane was so hungover he couldn't even remember a single thing Simon had just told him always makes me laugh.

### Top 7 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Potential Hot Take:  This is the best Die Hard film.

Sam Jackson is outstanding, Jeremy Irons is enjoying every moment, and it's one of the best NYC films ever.
  - There's something about it that really makes it stand out from other '90s action movies, as well as movies set in New York.
  - You know what, thinking about it, you may be right--even if it doesn't happen on Christmas.  I really like watching Samuel L. Jackson and Bruce Willis bicker with each other.  Jeremy Irons is great (as usual).  New York City is a more fun environment than Nakatomi Plaza.  There isn't any conspicuous use of the "HK slap" on a submachine gun or Steyr AUG, so it isn't perfect, but still a fun ride by any measure.
2. Why you keep calling me 'Jesus'!? I look Puerto Rican to you!?
  - "He didnt say JESUS, he said HEY....ZUES!!"

LOL cracks me up everytime
    - "Zeus?"

"Yeah, Zeus! As in, father of Apollo? Mt. Olympus? Don't fuck with me or I'll shove a lightning bolt up your ass? Zeus! You got a problem with that?"
3. When they cut through Central Park

“Jesus Christ McClane, are you aiming for these people!?”

“No!…Well, maybe that mime”

It cracks me up every time I watch it, the delivery is perfect.
4. “Being too hungover to chase a perp? That’s a classic old school move.” Jimmy Brogan
5. Vengeance is probably my favorite. It’s the one I watch the most. Jackson and Willis together are awesome. It’s basically a buddy cop movie compared to the other ones. It’s got great action and it’s hilarious. Jeremy Irons is stellar as well. Great villain.
6. The Sam Jackson and Bruce Willis chemistry is insanely good

## 4. Men, what is your favourite movie mainly aimed at a female audience?

**Score:** 454
**Body:** I’m a straight guy and I love the Devil Wears Prada since I’m a big fan of Anne Hathaway. 

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Does Thelma and Louise count?
2. Mean Girls, easy.
3. 10 things I hate about you
4. Clueless
5. Death becomes her.
6. Legally Blonde is incredible
7. Easy A
  - Such a good movie. Stanley Tucci’s scenes are great too.
8. I have Miss Congeniality memorised 
9. 9 to 5

## 5. Currently watching: BlackBerry (2023) starring Jay Baruchel, The Golden God. Directed by Matt Johnson

**Score:** 400

### Top 6 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Glenn Howerton was excellent, as always.
  - You might say he was a five star man.
2. Glenn Howerton needs more roles where he can flex his muscles like this. He steals every scene.
  - Hey! Get off the fucking internet!
    - WATERRRLOOOOOOO 🧛‍♂️
3. It was better than I expected.
4. Glenn being the one with the least success outside of Sunny when he's so talented is crazy to me
  - He had a good run on AP Bio
  - his 30 sec cameo in crank still holds up today, and it pretty much is 30-40% of his career outside sunny
5. So good. Not enough people saw it

# Top 5 posts from r/TrueFilm day

## 1. For those who wondered what happened to Andrew Dominik or whether or not he deserves to be in director jail after Blonde, he made an all-AI music video for Nick Cave (with whom he worked on a concert documentary).

**Score:** 154
**Body:** So the background to this is that Nick Cave posted a very scathing letter on his website about how AI is the death of human creativity (specifically because a fan wrote in with a "ChatGPT written Nick Cave song.") Cave [responded](https://www.theredhandfiles.com/chat-gpt-what-do-you-think/):

>What ChatGPT is, in this instance, is replication as travesty... this song is bullshit, a grotesque mockery of what it is to be human, and, well, I don’t much like it... I have got the fire of hell in my eyes – and it’s ChatGPT.

So he was insulted that someone thought a machine could reproduce his own inimitable artistic flair.

Fast forward to June of this year, and Dominik makes an AI music video of one of Cave's best songs (and one of my, well at least used to be, all time favorites), Tupelo. The MV features a bunch of grotesque AI versions of Elvis (the song is based on Elvis' mythos). I won't link it because it's just that devoid of any artistic merit but it's on the official Bad Seeds YouTube. It has all the classic hallmarks of shitty AI with weird hands, weird eyes, and Dominik was even too lazy to take a watermark out of one bit.

Dominik's reply [when asked ](https://www.clashmusic.com/news/nick-cave-has-decided-ai-might-be-ok-actually/)why AI? "It's a tool like any other."

So there you have it, the assassination of Andrew Dominik by the coward, his own lack of artistic integrity.

### Top 4 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. I was wondering if it was designed to be uncanny / uncomfortable/ lean into the grotesque nature of AI but it just seems really really boring? I tried to watch it and then skipped around a bit and it just seems so pointless. "Here's a childhood photo of Elvis I put in an AI program to make it look around." "Uh...ok?"
  - Yeah it's extremely boomer on FB coded... just an all around humiliating fall for Cave and Dominik both.
2. "AI is just a tool. You didn't even notice it. How is this different from CGI?" Etc etc etc. Unfortunately the slop peddlers don't even try to rationalize it anymore. They just don't care about art.
  - AI has revealed an ugly truth: so many people are artless. They don't understand or don't care about the process behind art, the reasons why it was made. It's just something to look at to fill the time. That's the mindset of everyone that watches AI videos and such. And now real artists I used to respect are being corrupted by this.
3. I'm more disappointed in Nick Cave here. I have a lot of respect for him and I love his music, but this is a misstep. It's possible this was done without his knowledge and I want to give him the benefit of the doubt, but unfortunately that's harder to do these days. I wonder what caused a change of heart considering how long he's been making music and how seriously he takes it

Edit: I just read his [Red Hand Files](https://www.theredhandfiles.com/tupelo-film-elvis/) entry about this. He truly found this video deep and moving enough to change his mind.

> To our surprise, we found it to be an extraordinarily profound interpretation of the song – a soulful, moving, and entirely original retelling of ‘Tupelo’, rich in mythos and a touching tribute to the great Elvis Presley, as well as to the song itself. The AI-animated photographs of Elvis had an uncanny quality, as if he had been raised from the dead, and the crucifixion-resurrection images at the end were both shocking and deeply affecting. 

I don't know how to feel about this. He was better than this. I didn't think so little would be able to change his mind
  - > a soulful, moving, and entirely original retelling of ‘Tupelo’, rich in mytho

😂😂

what a load of shit
    - It's just old pictures of Elvis made to move around a bit with AI. It's stuff my stepdad would watch on youtube and think it's real
  - Pretty simple: it appealed to his ego. The first one he found insulting as it was directly mimicking him, the second one follows roughly the same ideas behind  Tupleo and was made as a gift.
  - I love the man's music but he's sniffing his own farts here.
  - Nick Cave is just a boomer in the end

## 2. Scorsese’s Latest Films: Watching The Irishman and Killers of The Flower Moon Back to Back

**Score:** 7
**Body:** I had the house to myself this weekend and decided to revisit Scorsese’s latest two epics. My god, what an incredible bookend to his career. Watching both films across a 7-hour period, I just had to marvel at the richness of Scorsese latest two efforts — there’s so much to appreciate.

In The Irishman, the first 2 hours are engaging, but it’s really the final 90 minutes that transforms it into a masterwork. As the dilemma between Frank’s loyalties to the mafia and his friendship with Hoffa become more apparent, it’s impossible to look away. De Niro’s performances really sells the internal torment of his decision, while the amount of tension surrounding Hoffa’s execution is incredibly absorbing. In addition, the way the film is bookended by a meditation on mortality and reconciling with the decisions one has made, I was amazed at the amount of sympathy Scorsese was able to generate for a brutal murderer. 

Regarding Flower Moon, I was struck by the amazing directing and production design. Some of the shots of the environment, and the manner in which the panning is employed to capture the western vistas, make for a truly immersive experience. The production design and amount of detail is fairly astonishing, even just considering the costume design. And of course, it’s just such a thematically rich film. The inhumanity, the capturing of a culture, the contrast between capitalism and spirituality, one of the most perplexing cognitive dissonance of any character, and overall just such a harrowing experience to watch. I do think the film runs out of steam, unlike The Irishman which finishes strong. Still, a brilliant film. 


### Top 1 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Great idea! I might have to do the same! Truly feel that Scorcese’s done his best work with Wolf, Silence, and these two. Not always as fun as his earlier stuff, but the sweeping themes and subject matter about religion, capitalism, and American identity have been amazing in these pictures. 

Nobody really talks about how De Niro’s character is involved with the CIA and FBI at a few points in the Irishman, but all that corresponds with some prominent JFK conspiracy theories…fascinating.
  - Yeah, Silence is seriously underrated—a brilliant exploration of faith under the most difficult circumstances.

## 3. Searching for great father-daughter stories

**Score:** 6
**Body:** Stalled out about 40,000 words into a work in progress, which in no small part hinges on a close relationship between a father, and his adult, artist daughter (who has a physical disability) in the midst of mutual grief.

In an effort to jump start my process I’ve watched 

— “Alice in the Cities”, and plan to watch 

— “Aftersun” and 

—“Somewhere”

Any other recommendations?

### Top 3 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. The Wrestler and The Whale. (Does Arronofsky have a daughter of his own?)

Nope by Jordan Peele has an interesting approach to this theme with a storyline that's largely untold but heavily implied enough to create a catharsis at the end.

The Father with Anthony Hopkins and Olivia Coleman.
2. It's maybe not the central focus, but the father-daughter relationship is a powerful animating force in Evil Does Not Exist (2023, Ryūsuke Hamaguchi). Don't want to spoil but it's very important to the ending.

The newest Wes Anderson is actually along with OBAA yet another ___ Anderson-directed 2025 father-daughter movie featuring Benicio del Toro. If you have the gene that makes Wes Anderson movies enjoyable and not taste like soap to you, check it out, it's a lot of fun.

Titane (2021, Julia Ducournau) is insane but also at its core a very tender father-daughter story.
  - I have seen “OBAA” 4x—but I also loved the tenderness of “The Phoenician Scheme” too
3. One battle
After another certainly felt like a father daughter story after everything else. 
Leave No Trace by Debra Granik. 
I’ve heard that the new film “sentimental value” is centered on father daughter dynamic.
  - I have seen “OBAA” 4x in theaters 😂
    - Hahahaha! Amazing. That’s the way to do it!

## 4. FRANKENSTEIN - What is the role of suffering for the monster?

**Score:** 2
**Body:** Does the Creature become more human through suffering, or does suffering reveal he was always human? What actually defines humanity in del Toro's interpretation?

The Creature begins with gentle instincts - seeking warmth, attempting connection - suggesting humanity was already there. Even the way he says Victor was child like, even at the end.   

  
Would love your thoughts!

### Top 2 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Suffering taught him what the world was like. He also learned that it's unavoidable. Since life was first created, one living thing has to make another suffer, for food, therefore for survival. Humans have created a society where we can avoid making others suffer, but yet we still do because it's our instinct. However, it doesn't have to be. We should make it our goal as a species to overcome it
2. To me in the beginning, the creature is a blank slate, innocent like a new born child. And then he gets rejected, tormented and beaten by it's father first and later by others. But at the end it triumphs and doesn't get completely corrupted by mankind and his creator, the creature shows even compassion towards his tormentor Victor and breaks the cycle of Frankensteins Curse of resentfullness(esses??). Which makes him - the monster, a better human than Victor and most other humans in the end.

## 5. Animated Characters are better than Real Actors

**Score:** 0
**Body:** Biggest issue is that real actors carry a legacy, so when I see Timothy Chalamet, I am watching with memory of ‘Call me by your Name’ and ‘Dune’

And this baggage will keep increasing with more roles and real life persona.

That’s why it’s difficult for old actors to disappear in their character.

It’s difficult to not see De Niro, in anything new he does.

That’s why first impressions are best. And best moments are when you don’t know actor he becomes the first character that blew up.

Benedict as Sherlock for example, or Tony Sopranos or Cast of Friends.

Animated Characters begin and end with that film and franchise.

### Top 8 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. If you have seen as many animated projects I have you get used to their voices and I think it's even harder to suspend disbelief.

Like, oh that's just Gray DeLisle, Chris Sabat, Akio Otsuka, etc. Most of the time I find voice actors don't even try to change their voice while I find actors do so more often.
2. [deleted]
3. I’m going to ironize, but of course de Niro will never leave as much of an impression as Wreck it Ralph! 

But seriously, you should have provided some examples. The opposite happens all the time, when the legacy of the actor makes the movie better. See, let’s say, Travolta in Pulp Fiction or any surprising role for a given star.
4. This is a colossally bad take; it shows an inability to develop appreciation in the craft, but also demonstrates a woeful naiveté on your part that borders on selfishness and a demonstrates completely insular view of the medium. 

Benedict Cumberbatch doesn’t feel like Sherlock in *The Imitation Game* nor is there a real connective tissue with the Asperger’s/austism coded Holmes he plays and Doctor Strange as a successful, Randian professional who oozes self-satisfaction until his comeuppance. I could go on, but maybe watch more of his work and lay out the differences on a piece of paper to divorce your bias. 

Anyone who has seen *Band of Brothers* will be able to counter your Friends point with David Schwimmer. But also, why do you insist on comparing the TV and film examples. Gandolfini had 8 years and 86 episodes as Tony Sopranos. Probably 45 hours of screen time as the character.  Of course he’s more familiar than the characters he played in the second billing… but also, he’s amazing in *True Romance*. 

If you’ve ever watched *Futurama* for multiple viewings, you’ll find it hard to watch anything with John DiMaggio without thinking of Bender.

Be more critical of your habits before you try to make this about the entire medium.
5. I don't understand this post. When I watch a movie or show, my level of immersion is never gonna be 100% because I know that I am a movie or a show. I only need enough immersion to just enjoy or feel the work. That doesn't stop when a character is played by an actor I know, unless I am going out of my way to think about it. So it barely effects or enhances the experience. 
And animation can't do complex or subtle acting like you can have with real actors with real facial expressions. Like Al Pacino as Michael Corleone in The Godfather, or Joaquin Phoenix and Philip Seymour Hoffman in The Master for example.
6. You should take this to the next level and never sit in the same theater as you previously have, as that may conjure memories of movies you've seen before and ruin your immersion. 


Change your snacks up, who you sit with, and your clothes as well. 


Burn everything to the ground while you're at it. 
7. I think the biggest advantage that animated characters have is that the imagination is the limit with on-screen effects. You just draw/animate what you want. The limit is talent and ofc. time/money but not nearly as much a limit as it is when you need to make photoreal effects that blend into the same scene as real actors.

With real actors you are dependant upon the directors knowledge of how to truly capture effects and seamlessly blend them into the movie. Take Gareth Edwards - his knowledge of doing CGI work helps him do crazy good visual effects on lower budgets that other directors are simply incapable of. If only he was half as good at picking/writing a script he'd be a household name.

---
